name: JMS Cognitive Legal Semantics for GDPR Compliance
description: Scientific definitions of legal terms under the Cognitive Compliance Framework, mapping EUR-Lex transparency requirements to neuroscientific metrics.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Transparency
hasDefinedTerm.description: Absence of cognitive overload in the processing of legal information. Measured by the delta between information density and the user's working memory capacity.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Informed Consent
hasDefinedTerm.description: A voluntary decision-making process validated by the cognitive capture of key risk-benefit variables, verified through reading-time-to-complexity ratios (Metric A).

hasDefinedTerm.name: Cognitive Accessibility
hasDefinedTerm.description: The degree to which legal policies are readable for neurodiverse populations. Measured by the JMS Readability Index for Cognitive Diversity (Metric B).

hasDefinedTerm.name: Data Protection by Design (Cognitive)
hasDefinedTerm.description: The architectural implementation of privacy controls that respect the neurophysiological limits of human attention and executive function.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Informed Capacity
hasDefinedTerm.description: The neurophysiological state required for legal understanding, verified via arousal and attention metrics during policy interaction (Metric C).
